title: BLOOM 176B Model Training
description:
BLOOM (BigScience Large Open-science Open-access Multilingual Language Model) is a 176 billion parameter language model trained by the BigScience workshop.

Using a comprehensive life-cycle assessment methodology (as detailed by Luccioni et al., 2022), researchers evaluated the complete training process from hardware manufacturing through operational deployment, resulting in 50.5 tonnes of CO2e total emissions comprising embodied emissions from hardware manufacturing (11.2 tonnes, 22.2%), dynamic power consumption (24.69 tonnes, 48.9%), and idle infrastructure consumption (14.6 tonnes, 28.9%). Training consumed 433,196 kWh of electricity over 1,082,990 GPU hours using Nvidia A100 80GB GPUs on the Jean Zay cluster in France (57 gCO2e/kWh). Hardware embodied emissions were calculated by amortizing manufacturing footprint over 6-year equipment lifecycle at 85% utilization: 136,209 server-hours (48 nodes × 2,837.68 training hours) at 0.056 kg CO2e/h per server, plus 1,082,990 GPU-hours at 0.003 kg CO2e/h per GPU.

consumer.name: Infrastructure overhead
consumer.description: Datacenter cooling, networking, storage, and computing node idle power (46% of total consumption)

consumer.name: Server hardware manufacturing
consumer.description: HPE Apollo 6500 Gen10 Plus servers (48 nodes × 2,837.68 hours training time)

source.name: Embodied carbon
source.description: Manufacturing footprint of 2500 kg CO2e per server, amortized over 6-year lifetime at 85% utilization

consumer.name: GPU hardware manufacturing
consumer.description: Nvidia A100 80GB GPUs (384 GPUs on average, 1,082,990 GPU hours total)

source.name: Embodied carbon
source.description: Manufacturing footprint of 150 kg CO2e per A100 GPU (lower bound estimate), amortized over 6-year lifetime at 85% utilization
